about.support.title: Support independent open-source development
about.support.bullet1: This project is maintained using paid AI tools for development, debugging, and testing.
about.support.bullet2: When the AI usage limit is reached and more tokens can't be purchased, development pauses until the next billing cycle.
about.support.bullet3: If this tool is useful to you, your support directly funds more AI usage so fixes and improvements ship sooner without delays.
about.support.toast_body: If this plugin helps you, consider contributing to keep improvements shipping.

guide.summary.title: Guide
guide.summary.description: Help and usage notes for the editor modules
guide.intro: Use pose JSON files to build a Gallery, combine multiple poses into collections, and clean existing OpenPose data.
guide.section.where_put.title: Where to put pose files
guide.section.where_put.body1: Place pose JSON files in poses/ so the Gallery and pose selector can load them automatically.
guide.section.where_put.body2: By adding folders containing individual pose JSON files inside the poses/ folder, each folder will be loaded as a separate category in the pose selector.
guide.section.collections.title: Pose collection JSON files (Gallery native format)
guide.section.collections.body: A pose collection is a single JSON file that contains multiple named poses. This is the native format used by the Gallery and pose selector, and it's ideal for organizing and distributing curated pose libraries.
guide.section.collections.link: Learn more about pose collection JSON files
guide.section.import_export.title: Import / Export in the Editor
guide.section.import_export.body: Load or save a single pose JSON file directly from the editor for quick edits to one pose. Use Load to import and Save to export the current pose.
guide.info.auto_render_json: The OpenPose Studio node auto-renders poses from the JSON widget. Paste a valid standard OpenPose JSON and the stickman will appear without opening the editor.

pose_editor.areas.no_source.title: No conditioning areas connected
pose_editor.areas.no_source.body:
To see conditioning areas in the editor, connect this node's "areas" input to the "areas_out" output of the Conditioning Pipeline (Combine) node from the ComfyUI-LoRA-Pipeline extension by andreszs.

Do you want to install ComfyUI-LoRA-Pipeline now?
